title: When Considering a Popular Opinion: - Pause: Ask Yourself,
subtitle: Think Off the Bandwagon
description:
When considering a popular opinion:
- Pause: Ask yourself, "Do I agree because it’s popular or because it’s right?"
- Analyze: Break down the reasoning behind the opinion. Does it align with your values or evidence?
- Decide independently: Make choices based on your evaluation, not peer pressure.

Example: A team might agree on a subpar idea because everyone else does. Challenge it with better logic.

howTo.title: Pause and Reflect
howTo.summary: Take a moment to pause before agreeing with a popular opinion. Ask yourself if you agree because it's popular or because it's right.

howTo.title: Analyze the Opinion
howTo.summary: Break down the reasoning behind the opinion. Consider whether it aligns with your values and what evidence supports it.

howTo.title: Ask Clarifying Questions
howTo.summary: Pose a specific question that demands measurable evidence. This helps convert opinions into testable claims.

howTo.title: Make an Independent Decision
howTo.summary: Decide based on your evaluation. Choose to accept, modify, or defer the opinion, ensuring your decision is backed by evidence.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and reinforce the habit of questioning popular opinions. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/avoid-groupthink-decision-checklist/.
